label: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms

node.label: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms
node.definition: Continuous waveforms and time–frequency representations encode speech, music, acoustics, and sensor streams.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Waveforms, audio & spectrograms is to connect Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Sequences, events & time series to downstream capabilities including Multimodal, simulated & streaming data, Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding, Speech synthesis, voice & music generation.
examples: substrate for Multimodal, simulated & streaming data
substrate for Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding
substrate for Speech synthesis, voice & music generation
limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Sequences, events & time series.

dependencies.rationale: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Signal processing & harmonic analysis.
Waveforms, audio & spectrograms consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
Waveforms, audio & spectrograms consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
